title: Implement guest cart state orchestration
verify: First valid add lazily creates a cart and persists only the returned opaque ID.
Retrieve/add/absolute-update/remove state always adopts the backend cart response.
Reload restores by opaque reference, while malformed or not-found references are cleared without reconstructing cart contents.
Loading, empty, validation, stock conflict, and backend failure states remain deterministic and recoverable.

verify.closure_decision: Manual closure explicitly requested by the user after /verify PASS and per-task /red-verify semantic-pass.
verify.summary: Manual T2 functional verification passed and GENERAL closed TASK-022 after explicit user instruction. The required packet was ready and hash-matched; fresh cart-state tests, full storefront test regression, storefront typecheck, Memory Bank lint, strict doctor, and per-task red-verification passed. The state controller lazily creates/restores/mutates backend-owned carts, persists only eshop.cart.v1 opaque cart IDs, clears malformed/stale references, preserves backend failures for retry, uses absolute quantities, and stays within frontend cart-state/provider scope. FT-003 feature completion still requires later feature-level red verification.

Per-task adversarial semantic verification passed. The implementation fits TASK-022's bounded purpose as a deterministic guest-cart state boundary over the TASK-018 Store client/reference adapter: backend cart responses remain truth, browser storage stays reference-only, stale references clear without reconstruction, backend failures remain retryable, absolute quantity updates are preserved, and no cart page, product-detail, authenticated merge, OAuth, backend, checkout, order, inventory, or payment scope was added. This optional T2 per-task semantic-pass does not replace later feature-level /red-verify --feature FT-003 after all FT-003 tasks are implemented.

evidence_required: Reference-only persistence and stale recovery assertions.
Storefront typecheck and Memory Bank lint output.
constraints: Use the TASK-018 Store client and reference adapter.
Treat backend cart responses as rendered state truth.
Keep UI rendering and authenticated merge outside this slice.
invariants: Browser storage never owns items, quantities, totals, ownership, or availability.
A stale reference is cleared, not reconstructed.
purpose: Provide one deterministic guest-cart state boundary before rendering and post-auth integration.
success_outcome: Storefront state can create, restore, mutate, and recover a backend-owned guest cart without browser-authoritative data.
anti_goals: Do not implement cart page markup.
Do not implement authenticated merge or OAuth.
Do not cache cart payloads in local storage.

forbidden_scope: backend, checkout, order, or payment behavior
stop_conditions: The Store client cannot expose required backend response state.
Persistence would require storing cart contents or auth credentials.
Recovery cannot distinguish stale reference from backend failure.
